app.title: OpenSunstar
app.description: All-in-One Assistant for Claude Code, Codex & Gemini CLI

firstRunNotice.title: Welcome to OpenSunstar
firstRunNotice.bodyDefault: OpenSunstar lets you switch between multiple Claude Code / Codex / Gemini CLI providers with a single click. If you already had these tools configured, OpenSunstar has saved your existing setup as a provider named “default” so none of your previous configuration is lost.
firstRunNotice.bodyOfficial: An “Official” preset is also included in the list — click it any time you want to switch back to the official default. OpenSunstar automatically backs up your current config to “default” before switching, so you can move back and forth freely. That's how OpenSunstar works 😊

jsonEditor.mustBeObject: Configuration must be a JSON object, not an array or other type

commonConfig.guideTitle: What is a Common Config Snippet?
commonConfig.guidePurpose: It lets you share non-sensitive settings (plugins, environment variables, etc.) across different providers. These settings won't be lost when you switch providers.
commonConfig.guideUsage: How to use: ① Click "Extract from Editor" to save the common parts  ② Check "Write Common Config" when creating a new provider
commonConfig.guideReExtract: If you've newly installed plugins or hooks, re-extract the common config so they sync to other providers.
commonConfig.guideReassurance: Don't worry — your original configuration is safely stored in the default provider and won't be lost.
commonConfig.emptyHint: Click "Extract from Editor" below to extract reusable parts from your current configuration

claudeConfig.commonConfigHint: This snippet will be merged into settings.json when 'Write Common Config' is checked
claudeConfig.extractNoCommonConfig: No common config available to extract from editor

provider.noProvidersDescription: If you already have a config, click "Import Current Config" — all data will be safely saved in a default provider
provider.noProvidersDescriptionSnippet: Settings other than API key and endpoint (e.g. plugins) will be saved to a common config snippet for sharing across providers
provider.importFromClaude: Import existing providers from Claude Code
provider.importCurrentDescription: Import current live config as default provider
provider.blockedByProxyHint: Can't switch to an official provider while proxy takeover is active
provider.editProviderHint: Configuration will be applied to the current provider immediately after update.
provider.addProviderHint: Fill in the information to quickly switch providers in the list.
provider.searchScopeHint: Matches provider name, notes, and URL.
provider.managedByHermesHint: Defined in Hermes' providers: dict. Edit or remove it via Hermes Web UI.
provider.name: Provider Name
provider.configJsonHint: Please fill in complete Claude Code configuration
provider.editCommonConfigHint: Common config snippet will be merged into all providers that enable it
provider.usageSaveFailed: Failed to save usage query configuration

gemini.oauthHint: Google official uses OAuth personal authentication, no need to fill in API Key. The browser will automatically open for login on first use.

claudeDesktop.officialNotice: Claude Desktop official providers use the built-in 1P sign-in. No API key or endpoint URL is required.
claudeDesktop.modelMappingOffHint: Direct mode works only when the provider accepts Claude Desktop's three role IDs (claude-sonnet-* / claude-opus-* / claude-haiku-*); for any other model name (including legacy IDs like claude-3-5-sonnet-…), enable this switch to use mapping.
claudeDesktop.modelMappingOnHint: Claude Desktop only accepts the three role IDs claude-sonnet-* / claude-opus-* / claude-haiku-*. When enabled, OpenSunstar maps these three roles to your provider's actual models and keeps local routing running while in use.
claudeDesktop.routeMapHint: Fill in the actual request model for each of the Sonnet, Opus, and Haiku tiers. Use the menu display name for branded models such as DeepSeek or Kimi. Any tier left blank inherits the Sonnet tier's model (or the first tier you filled in), so the Haiku that sub-agents call is always available.
claudeDesktop.directModelListTitle: Manually specify Claude Desktop models (advanced, optional)
claudeDesktop.directModelListCollapsedHint: Native Claude model providers usually do not need this. Claude Desktop will fetch /v1/models automatically.
claudeDesktop.directModelListHint: Only fill this when the provider's /v1/models is unavailable or does not return Claude Desktop-recognized Sonnet / Opus / Haiku model IDs. Checking 1M declares 1M context support to Claude Desktop.
claudeDesktop.directModelInvalid: Direct models must use Claude Desktop-recognized Sonnet / Opus / Haiku model IDs
claudeDesktop.routeInvalid: Choose a model role and enter the requested model
claudeDesktop.routesRequired: At least one model mapping is required
claudeDesktop.statusUnsupported: This platform does not support writing Claude Desktop 3P configuration yet.
claudeDesktop.statusStaleRawModels: The Claude Desktop profile contains model IDs outside the Sonnet / Opus / Haiku role families. Newer Claude Desktop versions may reject it; switch to the current provider again to repair it.
claudeDesktop.statusMissingRouteMappings: The current provider has model mapping enabled but no valid routes. Edit the provider and add at least one mapping.
claudeDesktop.statusGatewayTokenMissing: The local routing token has not been generated yet. Switching to this provider again will write a new local token.
claudeDesktop.statusBaseUrlMismatch: The Claude Desktop profile points to a different URL than the current provider. Current: {{actual}}; expected: {{expected}}. Switch to the current provider again to repair it.

tooltip.active: Claude Desktop local routing is running - {{address}}:{{port}}
tooltip.inactive: Turn on Claude Desktop local routing for providers that need model mapping or format conversion. Configured address: {{address}}:{{port}}

notifications.openAIFormatHint: This provider uses OpenAI-compatible format and requires the routing service to be enabled

notifications.openclawModelsRegistered: Models have been registered to /model list

confirm.deleteProviderMessage: Are you sure you want to delete provider "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
confirm.removeProviderMessage:
Are you sure you want to remove provider "{{name}}" from the configuration?

After removal, this provider will no longer be active, but the configuration data will be retained in OpenSunstar. You can re-add it at any time.

proxy.title: Enable Local Routing
proxy.message:
Local routing is an advanced feature. Please make sure you understand how it works before enabling.

We recommend consulting the relevant documentation or your provider for proper configuration.

failover.title: Enable Failover
failover.message:
Failover is an advanced feature. Please make sure you understand how it works before enabling.

We recommend configuring provider priorities in the failover queue first.

usage.title: Configure Usage Query
usage.message:
Usage query requires a custom script or API parameters. Please make sure you have obtained the necessary information from your provider.

If unsure how to configure, please consult your provider's documentation first.

streamCheck.title: Model Health Check
streamCheck.message:
Health check tests provider connectivity by sending a direct API request. The following may cause check failures:

• Official providers (uses OAuth login, no standalone API Key)
• Some relay services (verify requests come from Claude Code CLI)
• AWS Bedrock (uses IAM signature authentication)

A failed check does not mean the provider is unusable — it only means it cannot be verified via a standalone request. Please refer to actual behavior within the application.

autoSync.title: Enable Auto Sync
autoSync.message:
When auto sync is enabled, every database change will be automatically uploaded to the WebDAV server.

This may result in significant network traffic. Please ensure your network and WebDAV service can handle frequent data transfers.

commonConfig.title: About Common Config
commonConfig.message:
The "Common Config Snippet" lets you share plugins, environment variables, and other settings across different providers — so they won't be lost when you switch.

How to use:
① Edit a provider → click "Edit Common Config" → "Extract from Editor"
② When creating a new provider, check "Write Common Config" (enabled by default)

If you've newly installed plugins or hooks, re-extract the common config to keep them in sync.

rectifier.title: Rectifier
rectifier.description: Automatically fix API request compatibility issues
rectifier.enabledDescription: Master switch, all rectification features will be disabled when turned off
rectifier.thinkingSignatureDescription: When an Anthropic-type provider returns thinking signature incompatibility or illegal request errors, automatically removes incompatible thinking-related blocks and retries once with the same provider
rectifier.thinkingBudgetDescription: When an Anthropic-type provider returns budget_tokens constraint errors (such as at least 1024), automatically normalizes thinking to enabled, sets thinking budget to 32000, and raises max_tokens to 64000 if needed, then retries once
rectifier.mediaFallbackDescription: When a provider does not support image input (it declares text-only capability, or the upstream returns an unsupported-image error), automatically replaces image blocks with an [Unsupported Image] marker so the conversation is not interrupted
rectifier.mediaHeuristicDescription: Predictively strips images before sending based on a built-in model-name list. When off, only the declared-text-only and upstream-error fallback paths remain, preventing the built-in list from misclassifying a multimodal model as text-only and silently dropping images

optimizer.title: Bedrock Request Optimizer
optimizer.description: Automatically optimize Thinking and Cache configuration before sending requests (only applies to Bedrock providers)
optimizer.thinkingOptimizerDescription: Automatically enable Adaptive Thinking for Opus/Sonnet, and inject Extended Thinking for legacy models
optimizer.cacheInjectionDescription: Automatically inject cache breakpoints at key positions in requests to reduce duplicate token billing

logConfig.title: Log Management

providerForm.fallbackModelHint: Used only when a Claude Code request does not clearly map to Sonnet, Opus, or Haiku. Usually safe to leave blank.

providerForm.advancedOptionsHint: Includes API format, auth field, and model mapping. Defaults work for most use cases.

codexConfig.localRoutingOnHint: Codex currently only natively supports OpenAI Responses API and GPT series models. If your provider uses the Chat Completions protocol or non-GPT models (such as DeepSeek or Kimi), enable this switch and keep local routing running while in use.
codexConfig.upstreamModelNameHint: For Chat format, enter the real upstream model here; routing converts Codex Responses requests to Chat Completions while keeping this model.
codexConfig.autoCompactLimitHint: Auto-compacts history when context reaches this token limit
codexConfig.modelMetadataAdvancedHint: Overrides Codex metadata for unknown models; clearing a field removes the matching config.toml entry.
codexConfig.modelMappingHint: Generates Codex model_catalog_json so /model can show these third-party model names; entries are saved exactly as listed. Codex must be restarted to refresh the model list after changes.
codexConfig.reasoningModeHint: Enable when the upstream Chat Completions API supports toggling thinking on/off. Providers like Kimi, GLM and Qwen usually fall into this category.
codexConfig.reasoningEffortHint: Enable when the upstream supports thinking-depth control such as low/high/max. Enabling this also turns on thinking mode and converts Codex's reasoning.effort into the upstream Chat parameter.
codexConfig.reasoningSectionHint: Preset providers are configured automatically; custom providers are inferred from name/URL. Override manually only when auto-detection is wrong.
codexConfig.advancedSectionHint: Includes local routing, model mapping, reasoning overrides and custom User-Agent. Enable local routing here when your provider uses the Chat Completions protocol or non-GPT models.

privacy.bannerShort: Local-first · keys in OS Keychain · no telemetry
privacy.title: Security & privacy
privacy.intro: Quick Connect follows local-first principles: data stays on your machine, secrets never land in config files, and network targets are yours to choose. Details below apply to the Quick Connect flow only.

localFirst.title: Local-first
localFirst.body: Supplier choice, model cache, CLI status, and usage summaries are stored in the app's local data directory. Before writing CLI configs, originals are backed up under simple-connect/backups; you can scan backups in Step 2 advanced settings.

keychain.title: Secrets in OS Keychain
keychain.body: API keys are stored only in the OS credential store (Windows DPAPI / macOS Keychain / Linux libsecret)—never in JSON, TOML, env files, or app logs. CLIs receive sc-local-* proxy tokens only; real keys are read from Keychain inside the local proxy process.

network.title: Direct to your chosen upstream
network.body: Model fetch, optional key verification, and upstream API calls go from your machine to the base URL you select. The local proxy (127.0.0.1:17172) listens on loopback only—for key pooling and CLI routing—not exposed to the public internet.

noTelemetry.title: No telemetry or account binding
noTelemetry.body: Simple Connect does not send API keys, chat content, call logs, or device fingerprints to OpenSunstar or third parties. Usage panels read local CLI session logs and local proxy counters for on-device viewing only.

yourControl.title: Risk items you control
yourControl.body: URL key import should stay enabled only for links you trust—disable it in Step 2 advanced settings. With custom suppliers, assess the third-party base URL and privacy policy yourself.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.subtitle: Three steps: pick a provider → enter key → activate
quickStart.officialDesc: Official providers require browser login or subscription auth. Complete OAuth in Provider Management — you cannot connect with a key alone here.
quickStart.keychainHint: Key is stored securely in the OS keychain (Windows Credential Manager / macOS Keychain), never written to disk in plaintext
quickStart.doneDesc: Provider configured and activated. You can now use it in Claude Code.
quickStart.doneDescApp: {{app}} is configured and set as the active provider.
quickStart.proxyRunningHint: Claude Code requests are forwarded via the local proxy (127.0.0.1:15721). Keep OpenSunstar running — closing it will break Claude Code connectivity.
quickStart.proxyRunningHintDesktop: Claude Desktop is routed via the local proxy. Keep OpenSunstar running — closing it will break connectivity.
quickStart.firstProviderHint: This is your first provider. Start the proxy in Settings if needed.

listenPort.label: Listen Port

maxRetries.description: Number of retries on request failure (0 ~ 10)

validation.addressInvalid: Please enter a valid IP address

settings.invalidPort: Invalid port, please enter a number between 1024-65535
settings.invalidAddress: Invalid address. Enter an IPv4 (e.g. 127.0.0.1), IPv6 (e.g. ::1), or localhost
settings.restartRequired: Restart routing service for address or port changes to take effect

takeover.hint: Select apps to route — once enabled, requests from that app will go through local routing

tooltip.active:
{{appLabel}} is routing - {{address}}:{{port}}
Switch provider for hot switching
tooltip.broken: {{appLabel}} is routing, but routing service is not running

failover.proxyRequired: Routing service must be started to configure failover
failover.autoSwitchDescription: When enabled, switches to queue P1 immediately and automatically tries the next provider in the queue on failures

failoverQueue.title: Failover Queue
failoverQueue.description: Manage failover order for each app's providers
failoverQueue.info: When auto failover is enabled, requests follow the queue priority order (P1 first). On failures, the system will try the next provider in the queue.
failoverQueue.selectProvider: Select a provider to add to queue
failoverQueue.empty: Failover queue is empty. Add providers to enable automatic failover.
failoverQueue.orderHint: Queue order matches the provider list order on the Home page. Reorder providers on the Home page to change priority.
failoverQueue.dragHint: Drag providers to adjust failover order. Lower numbers have higher priority.

autoFailover.info: When the failover queue has multiple providers, the system will try them in priority order when requests fail. When a provider reaches the consecutive failure threshold, the circuit breaker will open and skip it temporarily.
autoFailover.validationFailed: The following fields are out of valid range: {{fields}}
autoFailover.failureThresholdHint: Open circuit breaker after this many consecutive failures (recommended: 3-10)
autoFailover.timeoutHint: Wait this long before trying to recover after circuit opens (recommended: 30-120)
autoFailover.successThresholdHint: Close circuit breaker after this many successes in half-open state
autoFailover.errorRateHint: Open circuit breaker when error rate exceeds this value
autoFailover.minRequestsHint: Minimum requests before calculating error rate
autoFailover.failureThresholdExplain: Circuit breaker opens after this many consecutive failures, making the provider temporarily unavailable
autoFailover.timeoutExplain: After circuit opens, wait this long before trying half-open state
autoFailover.successThresholdExplain: In half-open state, close circuit breaker after this many successes, making provider available again
autoFailover.errorRateExplain: Open circuit breaker when error rate exceeds this value, even if failure threshold not reached
autoFailover.maxRetriesHint: Number of retries on request failure (0-10)
autoFailover.streamingFirstByteHint: Max time to wait for first data chunk, range 1-120s, default 60s
autoFailover.streamingIdleHint: Max interval between data chunks, range 60-600s, 0 to disable (prevents mid-stream stalls)
autoFailover.nonStreamingHint: Total timeout for non-streaming requests, range 60-1200s, default 600s (10 min)

proxy.stoppedWithRestore: Routing service stopped, all routing configs restored

httpHint.400: Provider rejected request format. Health check probe may differ from actual usage.
httpHint.401: API key may be invalid, or provider uses OAuth auth. Check failure doesn't mean it's unusable.
httpHint.403: Provider blocked this request. Some verify client identity; may work fine in actual use.
httpHint.404: Endpoint not found. Check base URL and API path.
httpHint.429: Too many requests. Try again later.

circuitBreaker.title: Circuit Breaker Configuration
circuitBreaker.description: Adjust circuit breaker parameters to control fault detection and recovery behavior
circuitBreaker.failureThresholdHint: How many consecutive failures trigger the circuit breaker
circuitBreaker.timeoutSecondsHint: How long to wait before attempting recovery (half-open state)
circuitBreaker.successThresholdHint: How many successes in half-open state to close the circuit breaker
circuitBreaker.errorRateThresholdHint: Open circuit breaker when error rate exceeds this value
circuitBreaker.minRequestsHint: Minimum requests before calculating error rate

instructions.failureThreshold: Circuit breaker opens when consecutive failures reach this count
instructions.timeout: After circuit breaker opens, wait this time before attempting half-open
instructions.successThreshold: In half-open state, close circuit breaker when successes reach this count
instructions.errorRate: Circuit breaker opens when error rate exceeds this value
instructions.minRequests: Error rate is only calculated after request count reaches this value

agentTooltip.sisyphus: Main orchestrator responsible for task planning, delegation and parallel execution. Uses extended thinking (32k budget) and drives workflows through TODO lists to ensure task completion.
agentTooltip.atlas: Main orchestrator (holds TODO list) responsible for task distribution and coordination during execution phase. Delegates to specialized agents rather than completing all work directly.
agentTooltip.prometheus: Strategic planner that collects requirements through interview mode and creates detailed work plans. Can only read/write Markdown files in .sisyphus/ directory, never writes code directly.
agentTooltip.hephaestus: Autonomous deep worker ("legitimate craftsman") inspired by AmpCode deep mode. Goal-oriented execution that launches 2-5 explore/librarian agents in parallel for research before taking action.
agentTooltip.oracle: Architecture decision and debugging advisor. Read-only consulting agent providing excellent logical reasoning and deep analysis. Cannot write files or delegate tasks.
agentTooltip.librarian: Multi-repository analysis and documentation retrieval expert. Deeply understands codebases and provides evidence-based answers. Excels at finding official documentation and open-source implementation examples.
agentTooltip.explore: Fast codebase exploration and context grep expert. Uses lightweight models for high-speed search. The scout for understanding code structure.
agentTooltip.multimodalLooker: Visual content expert that analyzes PDFs, images, charts and other non-text media, extracting information and insights from them.
agentTooltip.metis: Plan consultant that performs pre-analysis before planning. Identifies hidden intents, ambiguities and AI failure points to prevent over-engineering.
agentTooltip.momus: Plan reviewer that validates plan clarity, verifiability and completeness with high precision. Rejects and requests revisions until plans are perfect.
agentTooltip.sisyphusJunior: Category-generated executor, automatically created via category parameters. Focuses on executing assigned tasks and cannot re-delegate, preventing infinite delegation loops.

categoryTooltip.visualEngineering: Frontend and visual engineering category for UI/UX design, styling, animation and interface implementation. Defaults to Gemini 3.5 Flash model.
categoryTooltip.ultrabrain: Deep logical reasoning category for complex architectural decisions requiring extensive analysis. Defaults to GPT-5.3 Codex ultra-high reasoning variant.
categoryTooltip.deep: Deep autonomous problem-solving category with goal-oriented execution. Conducts thorough research before action, suitable for difficult problems requiring deep understanding.
categoryTooltip.artistry: Highly creative and artistic task category that inspires novel ideas and creative solutions. Defaults to Gemini 3.5 Flash high variant.
categoryTooltip.quick: Lightweight task category for single-file modifications, typo fixes, and simple adjustments. Defaults to Claude Haiku 4.5 fast model.
categoryTooltip.unspecifiedLow: Uncategorized low-effort task category for tasks that don't fit other categories with small workload. Defaults to Claude Sonnet 4.5.
categoryTooltip.unspecifiedHigh: Uncategorized high-effort task category for tasks that don't fit other categories with large workload. Defaults to Claude Opus 4.8 max variant.
categoryTooltip.writing: Writing category for documentation, prose and technical writing. Defaults to Kimi K2.5 model.

slimAgentTooltip.orchestrator: Writes executable code, orchestrates multi-agent workflow, summons experts
slimAgentTooltip.oracle: Root cause analysis, architecture review, debugging guidance (read-only)
slimAgentTooltip.explorer: Regex search, AST pattern matching, file discovery (read-only)
slimAgentTooltip.council: Multi-model consensus agent. Runs multiple read-only councillors in parallel, then lets the master synthesize the final answer.

methodology.title: Project Configuration Dimensions
methodology.subtitle: Methodology, Preset Orchestration, Custom Orchestration, Design Contract — four independent configuration dimensions. Use as needed, no sequential dependency. Framework detection is read-only and does not modify project files.
methodology.scanAllNoProjects: Add a project first to scan
methodology.noneDetectedGuidance: Recommend starting with flow-kit (pure Markdown template system) or spec-kit (spec toolchain). The review-only preset suits projects with an existing code review workflow—no framework installation needed.
methodology.noDescriptors: Framework catalog is empty. Please confirm the database is initialized.
methodology.detectionMatrixHint: Project × framework detection results (✓ detected / ? possibly used / ✗ not detected / — not scanned)
methodology.orchestrationHint: Independent dimension: Select a project, configure flow preset, modules, and stages, then export workflow.profile.json or flow-config.yaml. No need to scan frameworks first.
methodology.orchestrationNoProjects: No projects yet. Please add a project in the workspace before configuring flow orchestration.
methodology.orchestrationPickProjectHint: Pick a project from the dropdown above to start configuring SDD flow orchestration.
methodology.recipeHint: Independent dimension: Visual stage graph + freely combine stages and modules, export YAML+Markdown hybrid Recipe files. Can be used standalone without configuring other dimensions.
methodology.recipeNoProjects: No projects yet. Please add a project to your workspace first.
methodology.recipePickProjectHint: Pick a project from the dropdown above to start composing a Recipe.
methodology.designContractHint: Independent dimension: Choose a brand template or custom colors to generate DESIGN.md + DTCG tokens. Unrelated to SDD methodology — any project with frontend/UI can use it independently.
methodology.designContractNoProjects: No projects yet. Please add a project to your workspace first.
methodology.designContractPickProjectHint: Pick a project from the dropdown above to start configuring a design contract.
